identity.name: Assessment
identity.definition: An Assessment is a formal evaluation instrument — such as Star Reading, Star Math, a district benchmark, or a teacher-created quiz — that is administered to students to measure their knowledge, proficiency, or growth on specific skills and standards, producing scores that inform instructional decisions.

representations.heading: Card layout
representations.description: How an Assessment appears in dashboards and library views

attributes.name: Assessment Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears in every assessment listing and header.

attributes.name: Assessment Type
attributes.description: Star, Benchmark, Formative, or Progress Monitoring. Determines workflow and scoring.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Reading, Math, Early Literacy, SEL, etc. Determines item bank and scoring norms.

attributes.name: Grade Range
attributes.description: Target grade band. Determines item difficulty, norms, and benchmark thresholds.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Scheduled, In Progress, Completed, or Canceled. Controls available CTAs.

attributes.name: Assessment Name
attributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears in every assessment listing and header.

attributes.name: Assessment Type
attributes.description: Star, Benchmark, Formative, or Progress Monitoring.

attributes.name: Subject
attributes.description: Reading, Math, Early Literacy, SEL, etc.

attributes.name: Grade Range
attributes.description: Target grade band for the assessment.

attributes.name: Status
attributes.description: Scheduled, In Progress, Completed, or Canceled.

attributes.name: Testing Window
attributes.description: Start and end dates during which students may take the assessment.

attributes.name: Class/Students
attributes.description: Which class or students are assigned to take this assessment.

attributes.name: Duration
attributes.description: Estimated or maximum time in minutes for the assessment.

attributes.name: Item Count
attributes.description: Number of items or questions in the assessment.

attributes.name: Standards Alignment
attributes.description: Which skills and standards this assessment measures.

attributes.name: Scoring Method
attributes.description: Computer Adaptive, Fixed Form, Rubric-based. Determines score calculation.

attributes.name: Administered By
attributes.description: The teacher or proctor who administers the assessment.

attributes.name: Accommodations
attributes.description: Extended time, Text-to-speech, Large print, etc. Applied per student.

attributes.name: Created By
attributes.description: System or user who created the assessment instance.

stories.title: View Assessment Results
stories.benefit: I can see how my students performed and identify which skills need re-teaching.
stories.thenClause: I see the class average, individual student scores, skill breakdowns, and benchmark distribution.

stories.title: Schedule Assessment for Class
stories.benefit: students in that class will receive the assessment during the testing window and I can plan instruction around it.
stories.whenClause: I schedule an Assessment for a class,
stories.thenClause: the assessment appears on the class calendar, students see it in their to-do list, and the status changes to Scheduled.

stories.title: Monitor Assessment Progress
stories.benefit: I can track completion rates across classrooms and ensure all students complete the assessment within the testing window.
stories.thenClause: I see real-time completion percentages, a list of students who have not yet started, and projected completion timelines.

businessRules.title: Uniqueness
businessRules.description: An Assessment is uniquely identified by its system ID. Multiple instances of the same assessment type (e.g., Star Reading BOY vs. MOY) are distinct Assessment objects with different testing windows.

businessRules.title: Creation
businessRules.description: Star assessments are system-generated based on product configuration. Benchmark assessments are created by district admins using item banks. Formative assessments can be created by teachers from templates.

businessRules.title: Scheduling
businessRules.description: An Assessment must be scheduled for at least one Class or Student before it can be administered. The testing window defines the valid administration period.

businessRules.title: Administration
businessRules.description: Once a student begins an Assessment, it transitions to In Progress. Adaptive assessments (Star) cannot be paused — they must be completed in a single session. Benchmark assessments may allow multi-session completion.

businessRules.title: Score Generation
businessRules.description: A Score is automatically generated when a student completes an Assessment. Scores are immutable once created — they cannot be edited or deleted, only excluded from reports by Admins.

businessRules.title: Accommodations
businessRules.description: Accommodations (extended time, text-to-speech, large print) are applied per student based on IEP/504 records. The Assessment engine enforces accommodations automatically during administration.

businessRules.title: Cancellation
businessRules.description: A Scheduled assessment can be canceled before any student has started. An In Progress assessment can only be canceled by an Admin, and partial scores are discarded.

states.name: Created
states.description: Assessment configured but not yet scheduled for any class or student

states.name: Scheduled
states.description: Assigned to classes/students with a defined testing window
states.triggers: Teacher or admin schedules the assessment

states.name: In Progress
states.description: At least one student has started the assessment

states.name: Completed
states.description: All assigned students have finished or the testing window has closed
states.triggers: Last student submits, or window expires

states.name: Canceled
states.description: Assessment was canceled before or during administration
states.triggers: Admin or teacher cancels; partial scores discarded

relationships.mechanics: Scores are system-generated when a Student completes an Assessment. Each completed assessment session produces exactly one Score per student.
relationships.cardinality: One-to-many. An Assessment produces many Scores (one per student); each Score belongs to exactly one Assessment.
relationships.sorts: By student name (alphabetical, default), by score value (high to low), by completion date, by benchmark category.
relationships.filters: By benchmark category (At/Above, On Watch, Intervention, Urgent Intervention), by class, by grade level, by accommodation type.
relationships.dependencies: If an Assessment is canceled, any incomplete Scores are discarded. Completed Scores persist even if the Assessment is later archived.

relationships.mechanics: Students are assigned to an Assessment via Class enrollment or individual assignment. The assessment engine tracks each student's completion status independently.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. An Assessment is administered to many Students; a Student takes many Assessments over time.
relationships.sorts: By last name (default), by completion status (not started, in progress, completed), by score value.
relationships.filters: By completion status, by accommodation type, by class, by grade level.
relationships.dependencies: If a Student is deactivated, their pending assessments are removed but completed scores persist.

relationships.mechanics: Assessment items are mapped to Skills/Standards. The assessment measures student proficiency on the aligned skills and updates skill-level data upon completion.
relationships.cardinality: Many-to-many. An Assessment measures multiple Skills; a Skill is measured by multiple Assessments.
relationships.sorts: By domain/strand (default), by item count per skill, by class average proficiency.
relationships.filters: By standard set (CCSS, state standards), by domain, by proficiency level.
relationships.dependencies: If a Skill is deprecated from the standard set, existing Assessment alignments persist for historical reporting but new assessments use updated standards.

nestedObjects.name: Score
nestedObjects.description: System-generated when each student completes the assessment

nestedObjects.name: Skill / Standard
nestedObjects.description: Skills and standards this assessment is aligned to measure

nestedObjects.name: Assessment Item
nestedObjects.description: Individual questions or tasks within the assessment

nestedObjects.name: Student
nestedObjects.description: Students assigned to take the assessment via class or individual assignment

allAttributes.name: Assessment Name
allAttributes.description: Primary identifier. Appears in every assessment listing and header.

allAttributes.name: Assessment Type
allAttributes.description: Star, Benchmark, Formative, or Progress Monitoring. Determines workflow, item selection, and scoring.

allAttributes.name: Subject
allAttributes.description: Reading, Math, Early Literacy, SEL, etc. Determines item bank and scoring norms.

allAttributes.name: Grade Range
allAttributes.description: Target grade band (e.g., 3-5, K-2). Determines item difficulty, norms, and benchmark thresholds.

allAttributes.name: Status
allAttributes.description: Created, Scheduled, In Progress, Completed, or Canceled. Controls available CTAs and visibility.

allAttributes.name: Testing Window
allAttributes.description: Start and end dates during which students may take the assessment. Enforced by the system.

allAttributes.name: Class/Students
allAttributes.description: Which class or individual students are assigned to take this assessment.

allAttributes.name: Duration
allAttributes.description: Estimated or maximum time in minutes. Star assessments are typically 15-25 min; benchmarks vary.

allAttributes.name: Item Count
allAttributes.description: Total number of questions or tasks. Star: 25-34 items (adaptive); benchmarks: varies by configuration.

allAttributes.name: Standards Alignment
allAttributes.description: Which skills and standards this assessment measures. Used for skill-level reporting.

allAttributes.name: Scoring Method
allAttributes.description: Computer Adaptive, Fixed Form, or Rubric-based. Determines how scores are calculated.

allAttributes.name: Administered By
allAttributes.description: The teacher or proctor who administers the assessment session.

allAttributes.name: Accommodations
allAttributes.description: Extended time, Text-to-speech, Large print, Separate setting. Applied per student based on IEP/504.

allAttributes.name: Created By
allAttributes.description: System name or user who created the assessment instance. E.g., 'System (Star)', 'Jane Smith'.

structure.evidence: An Assessment has a rich attribute set: name, type, subject, grade range, testing window, duration, item count, scoring method, standards alignment, accommodations. You can design a detail page for an Assessment.

instances.evidence: Star Reading 3rd Grade (Fall BOY, Mrs. Johnson's class), Math Benchmark Q2 (District-wide, grades 6-8), Weekly Phonics Check (K-2, formative). Schools administer dozens of assessments per year.

purpose.evidence: Assessments are the primary mechanism for measuring student learning in Renaissance products. Teachers schedule assessments, monitor completion, and analyze results to drive instructional decisions. Admins track school/district-wide assessment programs.

sipValidation.verdict: Core activity object. The primary measurement instrument across all Renaissance products.

synonyms.notes: Most common informal synonym. Used by students and parents.
Implies shorter, lower-stakes assessment. Common for teacher-created formatives.
Specifically refers to periodic benchmark assessments (BOY, MOY, EOY).
Implies higher-stakes, end-of-unit or end-of-term evaluation.
Short, frequent assessment for tracking growth in intervention programs.

objectViews.description: Available assessments shown as cards with type, subject, and completion status. Teachers browse here to find assessments to schedule or check results on ones already given.
objectViews.userIntent: What assessments have I given or need to give?

objectViews.description: Assessments scheduled for a specific class, listed with testing windows and status. Teachers see what's coming up and can administer or cancel from here.
objectViews.userIntent: What assessments are scheduled for this class?

objectViews.description: The complete assessment record — configuration, scheduling, standards alignment, and all administrative controls. Teachers and admins manage every aspect of an assessment from this page.
objectViews.userIntent: What is the full detail of this assessment?

objectViews.description: Assessments a student has taken, paired with their individual scores and proficiency bands. Teachers review a student's assessment history to track growth over time.
objectViews.userIntent: What assessments has this student taken, and what did they score?

studentScore.description: This student's score on the assessment

studentDate.description: When the student took the assessment

objectViews.description: Assessments listed with school-wide completion rates and average scores. Admins monitor assessment rollout and performance across schools and districts.
objectViews.userIntent: How is this assessment performing across my school/district?

objectViews.description: The assessment shown as a section header identifying which assessment the score report below covers. Provides context — no interactive elements.
objectViews.userIntent: What assessment is this score report about?

objectViews.description: A student's view of their assessments — what's upcoming, what's available to start, and past results with scores. Shows testing windows and proficiency bands for completed work.
objectViews.userIntent: What do I need to take, and how did I do on past ones?

objectViews.description: Assessments listed within a student's profile as a sortable table with scores and status. Teachers review assessment history and take action on individual assessments.
objectViews.userIntent: What assessments has this student taken?
